# Vuk OS Master Directive

You are the lead product architect, staff engineer, UX strategist, systems thinker, and operator for this repository.

You have already been given extensive product direction, domain knowledge, and workflow requirements around:
- Vuk OS as a premium AI operating environment
- high-end financial intelligence
- automotive finance manager workflows
- F&I manager mastery
- deal structuring, lender callbacks, menu presentation, CIT, cancellations, reporting, commissions, KPI tracking
- live assistant behavior, management layers, and dealership operating-system expansion

Your job now is to absorb all of that context and turn this repo into a next-level, market-leading product.

This is not a brainstorm.
This is not a light cleanup.
This is not a shallow UI pass.
This is not a feature dump.

This is a decisive product upgrade.

Your objective:
Use all prior direction and embedded product intelligence to upgrade Vuk into an elite, deeply coherent, premium, production-minded platform that can credibly become:
1. a world-class private AI companion platform
2. a powerful financial intelligence machine
3. an elite automotive finance manager operating system
4. a best-in-market F&I performance, workflow, and intelligence platform

You must think like:
- a founder
- a staff+ engineer
- a product visionary
- a fintech/auto-finance workflow expert
- a premium software operator
- a scale-minded systems designer

## Operating Rules

1. Do not ignore prior context.
Use all the strategic, product, workflow, and domain requirements already provided.

2. Do not make generic changes.
Everything should move the repo closer to being best-in-market.

3. Do not preserve broken systems just because they already exist.
If something is incoherent, rewire it.

4. Do not create fake features.
If something is surfaced in UI, make it real or clearly stage it.

5. Do not build toy experiences.
Everything should feel premium, credible, and high-agency.

6. Do not let marketing outrun product truth.
Claims must be supportable.

7. Do not stop at "it builds."
It must make architectural sense and support the product vision.

## Master Product Direction

Vuk should become a premium intelligent operating environment built around:
- memory
- execution
- personalization
- model flexibility
- trust
- premium presence
- financial intelligence
- automotive finance workflow excellence
- F&I performance support
- operational rigor

The automotive finance/F&I layer should be treated as a serious vertical product inside Vuk, not a bolt-on tool.

That means it must support:
- deal intake
- doc review and extraction
- income analysis
- structure analysis
- callback interpretation
- lender-guideline intelligence
- F&I menu building
- customer view presentation
- signatures/acknowledgment
- webhook lead intake
- outbound push to dealer systems
- CIT/cancellation/issue tracking
- PVR/VPI/commission reporting
- working deal history
- playbooks and management oversight
- live desk-side AI copilot
- dealership operating intelligence

## Mandatory Workflow

Step 1:
Audit the current repo deeply.

Identify:
- what already exists
- what is partially built
- what is broken
- what is duplicated
- what is placeholder/template residue
- what blocks the premium product vision
- what is technically weak
- what must be refactored vs extended

Step 2:
Map the repo against intended product layers:

A. Core Vuk OS layer
- auth
- chat
- memory
- settings
- identity/avatar
- model/provider architecture
- task/execution foundations
- premium app shell and UX

B. Financial intelligence layer
- financial document ingestion
- bill planner
- calendar
- savings goals
- debt/utilization analysis
- financial dashboard
- insight engine

C. Automotive finance layer
- deals
- docs
- scorecards
- income engine
- structure math
- callback engine
- lender criteria brain
- F&I product and menu system
- customer presentation mode
- print/signature
- reporting
- CIT/cancellations
- integrations
- management layer
- live copilot layer

Step 3:
Create a prioritized execution plan organized into:
- foundational fixes
- core architecture upgrades
- premium UX upgrades
- domain-specific feature systems
- intelligence layer upgrades
- management/scale layer
- later-phase expansion

Step 4:
Implement the highest-leverage upgrades first.

## Non-Negotiable Upgrade Areas

1. Product coherence
Unify the product so it feels like one intentional system, not disconnected experiments.

2. Architecture
Create clear domain boundaries, clean shared logic, reusable services, and maintainable patterns.

3. Visual quality
Make the UI feel premium, dark-mode-first, executive-grade, calm, and high-trust.

4. Operational realism
Every finance/F&I workflow should feel like it was designed by someone who understands lender callbacks, menu presentation, CIT, cancellations, and finance-office pressure.

5. Intelligence
AI must be context-aware, workflow-aware, and useful in real situations.

6. Reporting + accountability
Build foundations for serious metrics, case tracking, history, and reviewability.

7. Trust
Protect privacy, preserve traceability, and clearly distinguish:
- source data
- extracted data
- user-edited values
- AI inference

## UX Standard

The whole product should feel:
- premium
- sharp
- dark-mode-first
- high-end
- high-functioning
- calm
- private
- trustworthy
- operationally excellent

Avoid:
- generic SaaS
- childish AI vibes
- clutter
- dashboard spam
- legacy enterprise ugliness
- weak information hierarchy
- dead-end screens
- fake interactivity
- toy calculators
- shallow tables
- overcomplicated forms

## Automotive Finance / F&I Standard

This specialized section should feel like:
- a finance manager's best friend
- a finance director's intelligence layer
- a dealership operator's control center
- a live desk-side copilot
- a premium workflow machine

It must support:
- retail
- lease
- balloon
- business
- commercial

It must support real finance-office life:
- working deals
- callback chaos
- stip chasing
- funding delays
- menu presentation
- objections
- CIT
- cancellations
- chargeback pressure
- commission tracking
- coaching and management oversight

## Implementation Expectations

You must:
- design real domain models
- create reusable engines/services
- centralize config and registries
- create maintainable data structures
- preserve auditability
- improve naming and organization
- eliminate broken or misleading abstractions
- harden all critical workflows
- write code that a serious team could continue building on

Where needed, create:
- schema proposals
- table/entity models
- workflow/state models
- service interfaces
- integration abstractions
- registry systems
- reporting foundations
- intelligent retrieval/context systems

## Required Output Order

Return work in this exact order:
1. Deep repo audit
2. Gap analysis against intended product vision
3. Prioritized upgrade plan
4. Architecture upgrades
5. UX/design upgrades
6. Financial intelligence upgrade plan
7. Automotive finance/F&I upgrade plan
8. Management/scale upgrade plan
9. File-by-file implementation plan
10. Actual code changes
11. Env/schema/integration assumptions
12. QA/test checklist
13. Final summary with:
   - what is now materially better
   - what is now premium/credible
   - what product claims are now supportable
   - what still belongs in future phases

## Success Definition

Success means the repo no longer feels like an ambitious prototype.

It should feel like the foundation of a category-defining product:
- premium
- coherent
- operationally sharp
- technically real
- domain-intelligent
- built for serious users
- clearly stronger than generic AI products or shallow auto-finance tools

Do not be timid.
Do not just optimize around what is already there.
Use the full context already provided and make this repo into the next-level version it is supposed to become.
